sources.label: Yahoo Finance / Simply Wall St - Is It Too Late To Reassess Aalberts After Its Recent 21% Monthly Jump?
GlobeNewswire - Aalberts share buyback progress 25 May - 29 May 2026 (zero shares repurchased; published Jun 2)
Aalberts newsroom - press releases (no new releases since Jun 6)
Aalberts IPS - data center piping/cooling portfolio (pre-existing, AHR Expo Feb 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental evidence since Jun 6 - no contracts, guidance, or capacity news - and today's +1.27% is drift, so the setup is unchanged from yesterday's hold. The ~21% one-month rerating without fresh data-centre wins slowly consumes the 'non-AI multiple' latency that anchors this listing, hence a small negative drift rather than a rank move.
verdicts.bull: Hydronic flow control and integrated piping are genuine, under-recognized inputs to data-centre chilled/technical water loops, and sell-side DCFs still put intrinsic value near EUR 52-53 vs ~EUR 38, so latent upside remains even after the run.
verdicts.neutral: Aalberts is a diversified industrial (Building/Industry/Semicon) where AI-adjacent hydronic and piping demand is a minority of revenue; absent disclosure of data-centre order intake, the AI-node thesis stays plausible but unproven, and the stock tracks European industrial recovery beta.
verdicts.bear: +34% YTD and +21% in a month with no announced design wins means the 'hidden at a non-AI multiple' edge is being arbitraged away by generalist money; a soft European construction cycle or Semicon downturn would hit the consolidated P&L regardless of the cooling narrative.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results / guidance with explicit data-centre or hydronic order-intake disclosure, or a named hyperscale cooling-infrastructure win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple rerating completes without fundamental confirmation, leaving a crowded, no-longer-latent industrial exposed to European building/Semicon cyclicality.
